paper_title: Genetic Analysis of Maize Using Diallel Crosses

methods.description: Field trials were conducted to evaluate parental and F1 progeny generations using a randomized complete-block design. Controlled pollination was used to prevent pollen contamination among genotypes.

steps.description: Field trials were conducted at the Dardanos Research and Application Center of Çanakkale Onsekiz Mart University, Northwest Turkey.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct field trials at specified research center

steps.description: Field experiments were arranged in a randomized complete-block design with three replications.
steps.searchable_summary: arrange field experiments in randomized complete-block design

steps.description: Controlled pollination was used to prevent pollen contamination among the genotypes.
steps.searchable_summary: apply controlled pollination to prevent contamination

methods.description: Protein and oil contents of maize kernel samples were determined using a near-infrared (NIR) instrument after grinding and sieving the samples.

steps.description: Kernel samples were ground in a laboratory mill and passed through a 0.5 mm sieve.
Ground samples were placed in a rotary sample cup of the NIR instrument, and then scanned between 1200 and 2400 nm.
A local calibration was used to estimate protein and oil contents of the samples using Infostar software.
steps.searchable_summary: estimate protein and oil contents using Infostar software

methods.description: Diallel analyses were performed to estimate general combining ability (GCA) and specific combining ability (SCA) using Griffing's methods and a fixed-effects model.

steps.description: Diallel analyses were performed via DIALLEL-SAS05 program using SAS Version 8.
steps.searchable_summary: perform diallel analysis using DIALLEL-SAS05 in SAS

steps.description: Griffing's diallel methods were used to estimate GCA of parents and SCA of hybrid combinations.
steps.searchable_summary: estimate GCA and SCA using Griffing's methods

steps.description: The t-test was used to examine the significance of estimated combining ability effects.
steps.searchable_summary: perform t-test on combining ability effects

methods.description: Spearman's rank correlations were computed to evaluate relationships between genetic components, and midparent heterosis (MPH) was calculated to assess hybrid performance.

steps.description: The PROC CORR statement of SAS package was used to compute Spearman's rank correlations.
steps.searchable_summary: compute Spearman's rank correlations using PROC CORR in SAS

steps.description: Midparent heterosis (MPH) was computed by dividing the difference between parental means and hybrid value by parental mean.
